name: agent-eye
description: Give AI coding agents a visible browser and dev-server control for autonomous frontend run-test-debug loops, with server-side permission controls.

commands.title: Agent Eye: Setup for Claude Code (write .mcp.json)
Agent Eye: Reinstall Agent Integrations (skill + MCP)

agentEye.browserChannel.description: Browser channel for Playwright. Empty uses the bundled Chromium (run 'npx playwright install chromium'). 'chrome'/'msedge' use your installed browser.

agentEye.logLevel.description: Log verbosity for the Agent Eye MCP server (written to stderr / the server output channel).

agentEye.showCursor.description: Show a pulsing cursor overlay in the browser so you can watch where the agent points and clicks (Playwright doesn't move the real OS pointer).

agentEye.slowMoMs.description: Slow down each browser action by this many milliseconds so you can follow along. 0 = full speed. Try 300–800 while watching.

agentEye.autoInstall.description: On first run, automatically install the Agent Eye skill (and register the MCP server) into detected AI agents' global config (Claude Code ~/.claude, Codex ~/.codex) so frontend tasks use the visible browser without any manual setup.
